entry.title: Re: Does project release workflow impact data migration o...
entry.content:
Dear @AIDream,

SAP does not explicitly document that Migration Cockpit bypasses the project release workflow. However based on SAP standard documentation the migration object is intended for the initial creation of Enterprise Projects, while the workflow is intended for the project release step. Therefore the workflow should normally not interrupt migration unless the migration or a follow-up action sets the project into a release-relevant processing status

What SAP does state is:

  • migration objects are for initial migration, meaning you create data with them and SAP also states that Migration Cockpit cannot be used to update data for Enterprise Project corrections/updates SAP points to the API instead
  • the Enterprise Project workflow is described as a workflow for releasing a project, with approvers approving/rejecting/sending back the release
  • in the Enterprise Project API changing the processing status is a separate operation (ChangeEntProjProcgStatus) which shows that status/release handling is treated separately from simple project creation

Summary:

Migrate legacy projects in a non-released status then test one sample release in QAS before mass load. That is the safest statement supported by SAP documentation

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2602.500/en-US/FSD_CE2602.pdf

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/988903b47d7040f6ac4ec02e44bb58e4/0bfec05444d040078447b06fa2e1fe43.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: BiWhy – Why a Diagnostics Platform Is Needed
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available for free to everyone at biwhy.net.

BiWhy – Motivation and Principles

A diagnostics platform is needed to simplify and automate diagnostics, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis.

In many enterprise environments, the required diagnostic data exists, but it is scattered across logs, metrics, configuration, files, databases, and multiple tools. As a result, root cause analysis is often slower, more manual, and more dependent on individual experience than it should be.

This article outlines some supportability principles for enterprise software and the practical approach behind BiWhy, where I also tried to apply them. These principles help make software easier to operate, troubleshoot, and improve.

The motivations and design approach behind BiWhy – principles I also tried to apply within BiWhy itself

Software supportability
Some of the principles behind supportable software include the following:

  • Measure everything.
  • Log effectively.
    • Log everything important.
    • Support on-the-fly verbosity configuration.
    • Use centralized configuration wherever possible.
    • Cut noise.
      • Remove worthless log entries.
      • Maintain zero error tolerance – a healthy system should be clear of error and exception logs. It is either healthy or it has errors; it cannot be both.
      • There should not be errors that can simply be ignored. Either fix them or do not mark them as errors.
      • Provide easy log filtering at the point of generation as a practical workaround for the two issues above.
  • Make key information easily available, as simply as possible, ideally in one click.
    • Measures
    • Logs
    • Documentation
  • Automate or semi-automate repeatable problem detection and troubleshooting.
  • Provide a convenient UI to analyze all of the above.
  • Ensure that non-trivial configurations are guided and easily troubleshootable at each step.

Good supportability means

  • Problems and performance can be addressed and optimized starting from the lowest level.
    • End users
    • Customer report developers
    • System administrators
    • Vendor support layers
    • Vendor developers

This reduces pressure on higher-tier support layers and can save significant support effort every day.

Part of those resources can then be redirected to further automation and supportability improvement.

  • Overall, it helps create best-in-class software in terms of functionality richness, performance, robustness, and total cost of ownership.
    • Early error detection, including regressions, facilitates new feature development and problem correction.
    • Performance optimization at all levels leads to broader overall performance gains.

Example

SAP’s success with ABAP systems was, to a great extent in my view, supported by open architecture: logs lead to code, the code is easy to debug and measure, and customers can see what is going on and participate in development.

BusinessObjects addresses supportability in many areas, but it still has important gaps.

BiWhy was started with the idea of helping fill those gaps.

entry.title: IOS and Android Version Details for SAP Mobile Start Users
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We are using SAP BTP Work Zone, standard edition, and SAP Mobile Start. We would like to know whether it is possible to view the iOS and Android versions of users who are logged in to SAP Mobile Start. Kindly let us know if any such feature is available in SAP Mobile Start.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: IOS and Android Version Details for SAP Mobile Start...
entry.content:
Regarding your query, please notice that there is no standard feature in SAP Mobile Start to directly view individual user data such as iOS/Android versions.

As an alternative, you could use Mobile Services User Data charts: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/usage/admin/user-data.html

For example, with SAP BTP Mobile Services analytics, you can access aggregated device information such as: 

- Platform (iOS / Android)

- Platform version (OS version)

- Device model

- App version

These are available in analytics dashboards (User Data / Sessions) and can be filtered/reporting-oriented.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, March 19th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 

  • ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/abap-developer-challenge-joule-for-developers-using-basic-trial/ba-p/14336191 

SAP Insider Las Vegas 

  • Event website https://vegas.sapinsider.org/  

Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition 

  • Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-sap-integration-suite-enhanced-edition/ba-p/14346638  

CAP Feb 2026 release 

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/2026/feb26  

Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers 

  • Post with links to both surveys https://www.linkedin.com/posts/christian-lechner-inthecloud_do-you-use-the-btp-cli-andor-terraform-activity-7435231498376482816-rjWW/  

DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI – Chat Completion documentation https://sap.github.io/ai-sdk/docs/js/orchestration/chat-completion  

CHAPTER TITLES  

00:00 Intro 
00:07 ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 
00:39 SAP Insider Las Vegas 
01:59 Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition  
03:10 CAP Feb 2026 release 
04:58 Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers  
05:56 DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

TRANSCRIPT

This is the SAP Developer News for March 19th, 2026.

[Sheena]
Hello, everyone. We are halfway through the March Developer Challenge on Joule
ABAP AI capabilities. And we would like to thank all of you for the amazing
responses we have been receiving for the past two weeks. As you might know,
week three is already live and we are focusing on RAP predict business logic to
create a sample code by passing a simple prompt. We have one more week
remaining and we are excited to hear even more great responses. Till then, stay
Jouled!

[Rich and Witalij]
Hey folks, it's Rich Heilman and Witalij here in Las Vegas for SAP Insider
2026. Today we did a two-hour hands-on session on Joule Studio, which was good.
Lots of good questions and tomorrow we're going to repeat that and we're going
to do a Joule for Developers in SAP Build and a Joule for Developers ABAP
edition as well where we'll show everyone how to use Joule to speed up their
development process. And you did a hands-on session this morning. Yes and I did
a session on how to use SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine Capabilities to build the
Joule augmented generation. Lots of people are talking about agents right now
but a lot of customers are doing RAG based AI applications so it was good to
refresh this content and as well to mention that we still keep investing in
some of the capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine like for example the
very recent new encoder model. Cool very cool yeah so we got a couple more
hands-on sessions and I think we're gonna get out here and get on to the next
one. So, bye for now.

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP developers. There is a new edition available for SAP Integration
Suite, which includes lots of goodies. This is the new Enhanced Edition. From
an AI perspective, it includes features that were only previously available in
the Premium Edition. For example, iFlow generation using natural language,
script optimization to analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript
scripts, API anomaly detection, and API traffic prediction to help you manage
your APIs. Apart from that, you also get access to the entire suite of
integration capabilities, an advanced event mesh broker for your event-driven
architecture scenarios, SAP Document AI to turn unstructured data into
structured data, the alert notification service for monitoring, transport
management service to move artifacts across landscapes without manual exports.
So, lots of stuff. Make sure to check out Tricia's blog post on SAP Community
to get all the details. Hasta luego. Hello, everyone.

[Ajay]
The February release of Capire documentation (*CAP) is out. First, we have an
exciting update. Live queries in the documentation. Yes, you have heard that
right. You can now run CDS queries directly in your browser. You can view the
query results along with the corresponding SQL statements. You can also edit
the query by typing in the box, making it your own personal playground to
experiment with, and no setups required. Coming to Node.js updates. Parallel
GET calls are now supported in the OData $batch request. For draft-enabled
entities, calculated elements can now be reliably used for values display in
the UI or for information in the UI behavior. Starting with @cap-js/sqlite
version Node.js SQL implementation by setting cds.requires.db.driver to node.
Coming to Java updates, important changes include using a reference as a value
for substring prefix or suffix in the contains startsWith or endsWith functions
is now rejected. Performance improvements have been made for requests related
to Fiori Draft list report "All" filter, deletion of inactive drafts during the
draft activation, and hierarchical selects. Tooling updates includes a new
query mode in cds REPL is available via the .ql command. CAP now supports
version 10 of ESLint. And for the Cloud Foundry, cds up can now be adjusted
using MTA extensions. For more details, visit the Capire documentation. Links
will be included in the description.

[DJ]
If you use the SAP Business Technology Platform, and more specifically, if
you're using the btp CLI or the Terraform providers to help you manage your
resources there, then your feedback is sought. The team has a couple of
feedback surveys open right now, running through April as well, so it's still a
little bit of time. One feedback survey for the btp CLI and the other for the
Terraform providers. This is a great opportunity for you to provide your
opinions and share with the team how you're using the tools and also where you
see room for improvement. Links to both surveys, which, as I mentioned, are
running sort of through April, are in the description. After this news episode
finishes, why not grab a coffee and head over and give your opinion right now.
Thanks.

[Kevin]
Did you know that you can configure an AI orchestration flow in AI Launchpad
and use it to configure the orchestration client from the SAP Cloud SDK for AI
within a CAP project? It allows you to configure templating, content filtering,
data masking, grounding, and more. You can download the configuration and read
it from file into your orchestration client or directly paste the JSON string
from AI Launchpad into your project. At any point, you can upload the
configuration back to AI Launchpad to make changes or directly change the
config inline of your project.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code – Technical Series – Feature Flags Service
entry.content:
Thanks Nata for a detailed explanation of the Feature Flag Service. 

It seems a nice strategy to use to control the release of features and functionalities on the fly without reverting code.

I do have a couple of questions:

  1. The example covers the functionality very well when its microservices involved. How would this work if there is UI involved or other interfaces that the service could interact with e.g. SAP BPA. How would you control UI elements visibility or process tasks from executing based on the feature flag ?
  2. In the example you use function which handles the logic. If we wish to do direct binding from UI to entities of the CAP service and use the default handlers, my guess is you would need to add logic for each entity to intercept the flag toggle. Is there an alternative approach?
  3. How would this work with a multi-tenant SaaS application ? Can we control the feature flags based on customers ?

  Would definitely love a deep-dive session looking at the complete app with UI, microservices, external APIs, interfaces etc. involved. Thanks once again for the lovely writeup.

Regards,

Melwyn

entry.title: Live session : April 14th - Agentic AI and CX AI Toolkit in Sales Cloud
entry.content:
Artificial intelligence is transforming how customer experience teams operate, and SAP’s CX AI Toolkit available with Joule Premium is leading this shift with powerful agent‑driven automation. In this blog post, we’re excited to highlight an upcoming one‑hour live session on April 14 designed to help you understand the agentic AI capabilities available within the toolkit — and why you won’t want to miss it.

What This Live Session Covers

In this expert‑led session, you’ll learn how the agentic AI capabilities of the CX AI Toolkit available with Joule Premium work and how different agent types can be designed using the toolkit. Rather than creating agents during the session, you’ll gain a clear understanding of how to create, configure, and test a Q&A agent, as well as the steps involved in designing an agent.

What You’ll Learn

Who Should Attend?

entry.content:
Dear @susana89,

Yes. In Public Cloud 2602 the function Check period only in app MMPV (Close Periods) is obsolete. This is confirmed by system message MM380: Check is obsolete. No check is executed. entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks
entry.content:
**Updated March 2026**

Welcome to our blog series focused on the SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity (BDC) Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks.

This guide provides a comprehensive, step-by-step approach to help you estimate the capacity units (CU) needed for your business case (before purchasing) or how you can distribute them across your solution components (after signing a contract) for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks.

This post belongs to a blog series with 5 parts: 

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks (this post)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Datasphere (coming soon)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator for SAP Databricks (coming soon)

What’s SAP BDC Connect? It is a service available within SAP BDC that enables secure, bidirectional, zero-copy sharing of data products between SAP BDC catalog and partner systems.

This service is not designed for replication scenarios. For these cases, organizations need to consider Premium Outbound Integration (POI) in SAP Datasphere.

As of February 2026, SAP BDC Connect supports integration exclusively with Databricks across AWS, GCP, and Azure environments. Additional integrations (including Google BigQuery, Snowflake, and Microsoft Fabric) are planned for release by the end of the year.

For SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, consumption is measured in Capacity Units (CU). The total consumption varies based on: A) Inter-Region Data Transfer and B) Processing Requests.

1. Inter-Region Data Transfer: Data volume shared to external data platforms in GB (Gigabyte). It only applies when the “region” is different. When we say “region”, please understand as hyperscaler + geographic region (for example: AWS US East or US10). If we have a cross-hyperscaler or cross-region scenario, this metric is considered. Check more details on the table below.

 

Scenario

Databricks  “Region”

BDC Connect “Region”

Is considered Inter-Region?

Cross-hyperscaler (different hyperscaler, same region)

AWS EU10

Azure EU20

Yes

Cross-region (same hyperscaler, different region)

AWS CA10

AWS EU10

Yes

Ideal (same hyperscaler and region)

AWS US10

AWS US10

No

2. Processing Requests: Blocks of API calls for data requests (1 block = 2500 API calls).

Example: In a cross-region scenario, a customer with a total data volume of 500 GB and 1,000 blocks of API calls would consume 147.50 Capacity Units (CU).

📌  Want to estimate how many CU you need? No problem! Below, you can find a step-by-step:

1. Open SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator.

2. Select your "Hyperscaler" and "Data Center".

3. Click "Add" and double-click "SAP Business Data Cloud Connect".

4. Enter the number of Inter-Region Data Transfer and Processing Requests. In this example, we use 500 GB of data volume and 1,000 blocks of APIs. 

5. The final CU / Month amount will appear on the left. 

Image 01

Image 02

👉  If you plan to provision SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, please follow this guidance. Important: Always provision the service in the same region and hyperscaler as your SAP BDC Cockpit!

📈  If you want to validate your actual consumption for SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, please check this step-by-step. 

Sources:

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Service Description Guide
  • Provisioning SAP Business Data Cloud Connect
  • Data Center Availability in SAP Business Data Cloud

entry.title: Re: Tracking Capacity Unit Usage for SAC and SAP Databricks
entry.content: Hello, you can use this article as reference to check consumption across BDC components - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-check-consumption-for-sap-business-data-cloud-bdc-and-its-components/ba-p/14257413

entry.title: Re: Minimum CU for Datasphere with BDC Connector?
entry.content: Yes, a Datasphere tenant is required, and it needs to have object store enabled, since it is this feature that enable the creation of custom data products, which we share through BDC and then Databricks. You need to have a Datasphere tenant with a minimum of 4151 Capacity Units (CU).

entry.title: Provide values for node created with CreateIf in CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

I've some rather confusing issue in CPI.

I'm creating a node "ContactComms" via createIf based on some conditions. I have 7 values in the queue for the createIf, one is "true" and is resulting in a node as expected.

Within node "ContactComms", there is a tag "Value". Although number of values provided in the queue for this is the same, I never get a value in "Value".

The result has no tag "value". I would expect one with mail ab-email@gmail.com

I am happy for any hints on this issues. Thank you very much!

Harry

 

EDIT: As I wrote, I came to the conclusion. For value, "splitByValue" is the right function to use instead of "removeContext"

entry.title: What’s New, Now Relevant: Introducing Release Center
entry.content:
Every six months, SAP SuccessFactors releases a package of innovation: new features, enhancements, and capabilities that drive your HR practice forward. Change comes at a cost, however, and we know it. You have six weeks to understand, adopt, and test everything in the package. It’s a lot to manage in a short time. You told us. We heard you.

Through your feedback, SAP SuccessFactors designed a new experience to alleviate the burden of change management. It helps you find the new features, enhancements, and capabilities that matter to you, your tenant, and your HR practice.

We call it Release Center.

The Limits of Today’s Release Experience

The What’s New Viewer runs on the SAP Help Portal. It hosts the list of all changes across SAP SuccessFactors. But because it runs outside of SAP SuccessFactors, it can’t know your tenant, so when you open the page, it shows you all changes to all SAP SuccessFactors products. It can’t intelligently filter itself what matters for your tenant:

  • When you first open the page, you see all changes, not just those that apply to you. To see the changes that apply to your HR practice and tenant, you use your deep product knowledge to filter the list.
  • It’s hard to personalize the list for your HR Practice and tenant, and it’s even harder to share it with your team. Often, a spreadsheet is the best answer, but it’s not dynamically updated when we must update an article.
  • SAP SuccessFactors can’t build leading practices into a workflow experience.

Importantly, the What’s New Viewer on the Help Portal isn’t going away. It still runs the subscription service, it’s still the easiest way to share content publicly, and it’s embedded with the rest of SAP’s products.

Release Center: Breaking Through the Limits

Release Center is a new experience in the Admin Center. It starts our transformation from the current SAP Portal experience to guided, intelligent, and collaborative experiences.

We’re proud to focus on our Administrators’ experiences and give you tools to make your work faster, easier, and more personalized.

Release Center is Tenant Aware

Release Center automatically understands your enabled products and modules and your configured features so that it can surface the release highlights that matter to your tenant.

Release Center has Personalized Impact Intelligence

Not all updates impact your tenant or your HR practice equally. Release Center separates what is critical for your usage of SAP SuccessFactors because it knows how you use it. You can focus critical issues that require immediate attention for your configuration and leave lower priority changes for later.

Release Center Handles End-to-End Release Management

For the first time, release change management is a managed workflow inside SAP SuccessFactors. You can:

  • Assign release highlights to specific administrators
  • Track progress on individual articles with statuses like In Progress or Completed
  • Collaborate with others by adding comments and discussions to articles
  • Reduce dependency on offline tools like spreadsheets

Release Center is Designed with You and for You

Release Center isn’t just another tool. It’s the result of extensive collaboration with administrators and partners. We listened to you when you told us:

  • “Help me focus on what matters”
  • “Show me impact, not just information”
  • “Give me a way to manage releases with my team”

Our first version is built directly from that feedback, and it’s just the beginning. We can’t wait to hear what you think of Release Center and how we can relentlessly improve it.

What’s Next

We are actively investing in:

  • Deeper intelligence and recommendations
  • Expanded collaboration capabilities
  • Enhanced analytics and reporting
  • Broader coverage across the suite

Your feedback is critical. It shapes what we do next.

Starting your Journey with us in Release Center

We invite you to explore Release Center in the 1H 2026 release, integrate it into your release processes, and share your feedback through Influence channels and surveys.

Because a release process shouldn’t just inform you—they should empower you.

entry.title: Fiori Launchpad (WEBGUI) Default Download Path
entry.content:
Currently, the default Download Path of Fiori App (WEBGUI) is "Z:\".    How could to change it to some other download path?   


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad (WEBGUI) Default Download Path
entry.content:
Hello,

As per 2636752 - WebGUI File Browser: How to download files to C:\ instead of Z:\

Only Z:\ drive is available as download path for WEBGUI, this is expected behavior.

Meanwhile, with the fix of Note 3092845 Webgui FSM: download all files also to the native file system, all files that are downloaded to the z drive are also downloaded to the native file system.

The native file system refers to the download path defined in browser settings. See also KBA 3231710 - WebGUI File Browser: How to change download path while downloading to local drive (native file system)

It is also possible to export files to the native file system manually. Refer to "File Export" described in KBA 2249454 - ITS Up/Down: Using the WEBGUI File Browser.

entry.title: How do you gracefully withdraw CDs for managed landscapes that are already decommissioned?
entry.content:
We are running Solution Manager 7.2 (SP14).  A handful of landscapes previously managed by ChaRM were recently decommissioned and now no longer exist.  Unfortunately, the team that performed the decommissioning activities did not close all of the open change documents for these landscapes.  I want to gracefully withdraw all of the open CDs for these landscapes.  Of course, I can't withdraw any CDs for these landscapes in the ChaRM webUI if they had transport requests assigned.

I could use program CRM_SOCM_SERVICE_REPORT to unconditionally withdraw these CDs, but I would like to avoid that if possible.  I'd like to somehow remove the TRs from the CDs and withdraw them from the webUI.  I can't use a program like /TMWFLOW/TRANSPORT_DELETE to remove the TRs from the CDs because the RFCs can't communicate with systems that no longer exist.

Is there any possibility to accomplish this or is my only option to unconditionally withdraw the CDs?

entry.title: Re: Question About Certification Requirement for Access t...

Hi @Takuma_Masushige,

If you are a customer seeking access to Provisioning, please note that it is not granted to customers, even if you hold certifications. Provisioning is a shared backend across all SuccessFactors modules though relevant module certification is required. Please refer to the KBA 2273365 for more details.

entry.title: Allow Calibration sessions to refresh subject data from source (People Profile/PM form)
entry.content:
Use Case / Problem

Currently, when Potential (and other trend elements) are updated in People Profile after a Calibration session is activated, deactivating and reactivating the session does not pull the latest values. This leads to stale or inconsistent data within the session and risks inaccurate calibration outcomes, particularly in time‑sensitive cycles (Talent Planning, assessments, and immediate calibration afterward). SAP Help describes session status changes (activate, finalize, reopen), but there is no supported mechanism to re-sync source data without rebuilding the session.

Created an Enhancement Request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/369775/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Elisângela Martins
entry.content:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Elisângela Martins

Esta publicação faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
A ideia da série está neste post de referência: 🎙️ Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curit... - SAP Community

Fui convidado(a) por Simone Azevedo para participar da série
Somos SAP Community Curitiba.

Você pode conferir o post dela: About SimoneAzevedo - SAP Community

👩‍⚕️Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

Sou Elisângela Martins, consultora SAP FI. Venho utilizando a comunidade no meu dia a dia para buscar soluções técnicas, além de aprendizagem, capacitação e acompanhamento das inovações.

 O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
O que mais me motiva é o fato das pessoas trazerem sempre novidades e compartilharem seus conhecimentos, pois a comunidade é um grande repositório de respostas para dúvidas sobre todos os módulos.

Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Após um período difícil de pandemia, gestação e mudança de Estado (Rio de Janeiro-RJ para Navegantes-SC), sem rede de apoio, acabei ficando desalocada. Realizei diversas buscas e participações em entrevistas sem sucesso, já desanimada, surgiu a oportunidade de participar do evento do SAP INSIDE TRACK Curitiba 2024!

Fiquei muito feliz, pois vislumbrei uma ótima oportunidade de me reconectar com as pessoas da área, me atualizar com as novas tecnologias e tendências do mercado SAP.

Chegando no evento me surpreendi positivamente com a organização, atenção dos curadores e o alto nível de conhecimento dos palestrantes trazendo assuntos importantes para nossa área. E isto, fez com que eu me sentisse VIVA, PERTENCENTE e MOTIVADA!🏆💪🌱

Esta foto, representa muito para mim, eu fiquei muito emocionada nesse dia, pois a minha família me acompanhou, acolheu e me deu muita força para seguir em frente!❤️🙏

Que conselho você daria para quem está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?

A SAP Community é vital para nós consultores, desenvolvedores e parceiros SAP, onde nos conectamos com pessoas para compartilhar conhecimentos, resolver problemas técnicos, além de poder acompanhar as inovações da SAP em tempo real.

     Conecte-se comigo

LinkedIn 👉 www.linkedin.com/in/elisângela-martins-18b42321

Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido:

a compartilharem suas histórias na comunidade.

Além das perguntas da série:

 Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

 O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?

 Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

E deixo também uma pergunta adicional:

O que vocês gostariam de ter sabido antes de participar do primeiro evento da SAP Community?

 Frase final

“Cada experiência compartilhada é um passo a mais na construção de uma comunidade mais forte e colaborativa.”

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

Title: ErrorMessage: Could not open app. Please try again later.Details: Error: adding element with duplicate id 'com.sap.spa.inbox-content'Error: Error: adding element with duplicate id 'com.sap.spa.inbox-content' at i (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:535:424) at c.e [as register] (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:433:709) at https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:421:2670 at c.constructor (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:421:2872) at c.constructor (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:517:1377) at c.constructor (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/ui/core/library-preload.js:550:1101) at new c (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-0.js:439:4578) at https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.145.0/resources/sap/fiori/appruntime-min-3.js:261:15455 at new Promise (<anonymous>) at j.createApplication

Under content manager in build work zone I have added the HTML5 version of My Inbox as well as the local copy. On my workzone site I have only the local copy visible. I have this working just fine in our dev environment, however in production it is saying there is a duplicate ID issue.

Laura G.

entry.title: Reversing a Completed Outbound Delivery in EWM: How to Return Stock to Its Original Bin?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to understand the logic behind reversing a completed outbound delivery within the 3BS scope item. The process involves two steps, and I have a question regarding the second one.

Step 1 – Reverse the Post Goods Issue (PGI)
This step is clear and logical. After reversing the PGI, the stock is returned to the GI-AREA, which makes sense.

Step 2 – Cancel the Picking or better named Releasing the Stock.
This is where I'm running into confusion, both technically and logically.

From a technical standpoint, the reversal process is not explicitly documented within scope item 3BS, as it does not reference a picking task reversal, instead, it only describes a stock release. It was through KBA 3638009 – "How to Cancel the Picking of Stock in Manage Outbound Delivery Orders" that I found this is actually how the process is "cancelled", even though it is not a true cancellation, since it's only a stock release.

My question:
After successfully reversing the PGI and releasing the picked stock, the inventory remains in the GI-AREA, why?. Is there a way to automatically move it back to its original source bin, or is a manual warehouse task required?

Any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Joule Skill for S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Communication Arrangement uses Technical User, that has no relationship to the Business user or his roles. Joule uses Communication Arrangement to connect to S/4HANA, which uses a Technical User. Thus, Joule will not have any idea on which roles the Business User has.

If you are infact a 100% sure that what you suggests works in the system, then, please share the details of how your Communication Arrangement is setup.

entry.title: Re: Anzahl der Prozesse in CALM sind nicht richtig angezeigt
entry.content:
Hello Leyla,

The values of the workprocess-related metrics represent the average over a certain period of time, so they are accurate to two decimal places. This ensures the accuracy of the monitoring values. At present, there is no way to round it off to a whole number.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Direct GR into Special Stock O for Different Vendor
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a business scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud regarding subcontracting and direct delivery:

We would like to purchase raw materials from Vendor A, but instead of receiving them into our own plant stock, we want the goods to be delivered directly to Vendor B (subcontractor), and posted into special stock O (subcontracting stock) under Vendor B.

Our expectation is:

  • Create a purchase order to Vendor A 
  • Upon goods receipt, directly post the stock into special stock O for Vendor B
  • Later, use this stock for subcontracting consumption when receiving finished goods from Vendor B

Based on the above, we would like to clarify:

  1. Is it possible in standard S/4HANA Cloud to post goods receipt from Vendor A directly into special stock O for a different vendor (Vendor B)?
  2. If yes, what is the correct configuration or process ?
  3. If not, is the only supported approach to first receive into our own stock and then transfer to subcontracting stock via movement 541? 
  4. Alternatively, are there any other recommended approaches or best practices for this scenario?

Any guidance or best practice would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: ✨ SAP Talk Women CWB 2026: um encontro que se sente, se vive e se leva pra vida
entry.content:
A segunda edição do SAP Talk Women CWB foi daquelas experiências que a gente não explica… a gente sente. 


Realizado na Hotmilk, por meio da parceria com a PUCPR, vivemos uma noite especial no espaço Innosphera — um ambiente que respira tecnologia, inovação e, acima de tudo, conexão.


Desde a chegada, o cuidado já era visível: nossa voluntária @TalisMoraes  recebeu cada participante com um sorriso no rosto, entregando uma rosa e um cartão de boas-vindas .

Um gesto simples, mas cheio de significado — porque cada mulher ali importa.
A noite começou com uma abertura acolhedora, onde compartilhei a história do evento — que nasceu com um propósito muito claro: conectar, inspirar e fortalecer mulheres no ecossistema SAP e tecnologia.


E foi exatamente isso que vivemos.
A dinâmica de pintura de taças trouxe leveza, criatividade e conexão.
Cada participante recebeu uma taça como um brinde especial do evento — e ali, entre pincéis, cores e conversas, vimos novas conexões surgirem e momentos genuínos de troca acontecerem de forma leve e espontânea.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Tivemos a honra de receber @antonia_zorn , da SAP Community Global, que nos trouxe uma mensagem poderosa sobre comunidade.
Ela falou sobre coragem — de pedir ajuda, de ajudar e de se colocar.
E, com ainda mais significado, realizou uma de suas primeiras palestras em português, nos inspirando pela sua autenticidade.


Na sequência, @sil_fumura2026 nos conduziu a uma reflexão profunda.
Com dados e histórias, mostrou o quanto o universo da tecnologia é vasto e cheio de oportunidades — e o quanto nós, mulheres, ainda temos espaço para crescer e ocupar.
Reforçou que acreditar no nosso potencial não é opcional — é necessário.
E que, juntas, ampliamos nossas vozes, nossos espaços e nossos resultados.

 

 

Nossos patrocinadores também compartilharam suas histórias e reforçaram seu compromisso com o fortalecimento das mulheres na tecnologia — caminhando lado a lado com esse movimento.

 

CHPRO | CVA | LUPUS | SMARTONE

Ao longo do evento, experiências especiais tornaram a noite ainda mais completa:
ADCOS Dermocosméticos, proporcionando momentos de cuidado e conexão com o autocuidado
Aesthi, com quick massage, trazendo pausas de relaxamento em meio à energia do evento
Morana, com a exposição de semijoias, trazendo mais uma opção de experiência para quem quisesse aproveitar um momento de leveza, estilo e expressão pessoal


Encerramos com o sorteio de brindes, oferecidos com carinho por empresas parceiras

e finalizamos com a nossa foto oficial — com sorrisos, brilho nos olhos e a certeza de que vivemos algo especial 

 

 

 

 

 

 

A segunda edição foi um sucesso.
E ela só aconteceu graças às nossas patrocinadoras, apoiadores e todas as pessoas que acreditam nesse propósito.


Um agradecimento especial também às voluntárias, que estiveram presentes em cada detalhe, acolhendo, organizando e cuidando de tudo com muito carinho — vocês fizeram toda a diferença 

Talis, Priscila, Simone, Natacha e Beatriz

Mas, principalmente, graças a cada mulher que esteve presente.
Vocês são o coração desse evento.
Obrigada por se permitirem estar, trocar, se abrir, aprender e inspirar.
É essa energia que transforma um evento em um movimento.

 E se você não esteve conosco este ano… fica aqui o convite:

em março de 2027, esperamos você para viver tudo isso com a gente. 

Conecte-se com a SAP Community Curitiba
Quer acompanhar as novidades da comunidade ou participar das próximas iniciativas?
Siga nossos canais:

LinkedIn

Instagram

Grupo WhatsApp da comunidade

entry.title: SFTP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I understand in SAP Datasphere (SAP BDC) Trail version we can not create SFTP connection. How ever I tried to prepare steps in sequence as prerequisites to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere . Hope this will be helpful for understanding purpose.

I request community members, to let me know if I have any mistakes in below steps and if there is any way to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere in trail version.

From your Laptop Start Menu à Operational features

 

 

Check OpenSSH Client might already available

 

To install SSH Server click on Add a features

In search bar type Open --> OpenSSH Server --> Install

 

 

Goto Control Panel --> Click on Windows Defender Firewall --> Advanced Settings --> Inbound rules --> New Role

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From search bar search for Services

Try to find OpenSSH Server --> Right Click Properties

General Tab --> Startup Type = Automatic and click on Start        

Apply , OK and close window

 

 

 

Install WinSCP

From Google download WinSCP for windows --> go for official website

Run the .exe file for all users

Go with Default installation (Typical installation, Comander , install)

Select only check box Launch WinSCP --> Finish

As it will launch WinSCP

File Protocol = SFTP

 

SAP Cloud Connector

Log in SAP Cloud Connector: https://localhost:8443/

Add Subaccount

Goto Cloud and you can leave Host, Port, User and Password as blank

Click on Next

 

Configure Manually

 

 

 

 

In trail Version you can select Region as US East (VA) – AWS only. Other wise you can select any region. I copied Region and Subaccount from SAP BTP Cockpit as shown below screen shot. Normally in paid version you can select as per license

Login E-Mail/ID – This is of SAP BTP and Password also BTP.

 

SAP BTP Cockpit

 

 

Log on to SAP Cloud Connector à Select Subaccount crated in SAP Cloud Connector

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud Connector à SFTP

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How to get Host Key

Before Going to SAP Datasphere,

Goto Windows Power Shell and type below syntax

ssh-keyscan -p 22 sftp.XXX.XXX.X.X. hostkey.txt (in place of XXX.XXX.X.X – give your IP address)

Then it will open a notepad with Key.

But you need to take the last line in genearted Host Key from the note pad as like below

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

SAP Datasphere à Connections

 

 

 

 

 

Enter Host Key and give WinSCP Host details (SFTP)

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

 

 

We get below error in Free Trail Version

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Missing Steps

In SAP Datasphere (BDC) Trail version we don’t have access to System à Administration

 

 

 

If it gets success

Datasphere → BTP → Cloud Connector → SFTP is working.

entry.title: Modernization and simplification of SAP EWM on SAP S4 HANA 2025 FPS01 Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
With S4 HANA EWM 2025 FSP01,  below Functional enhancements are part of SAP Extended Warehouse Management for use with SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 Version

  • Support consumption posting (movement type 241) against assets
  • Specify the handling unit number during synchronous production and process order consumption
  • Receive mixed handling units through radio frequency transactions
  • Extend the stock list during warehouse task creation
  • Support alternative units of measure for the warehouse stock migration object in SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit 

Support consumption posting (movement type 241) against assets: Prior to S4 HANA 2025 FPS01, asset-related fields were not available  in delivery structures (LIPS) or passed through the delivery creation interface (KOMDLGN). 

Earlier the delivery items which are stored in the table LIPS, which does not contain an asset field, however the asset information is mandatory when posting 241 movement. As a result the asset number is not read by function module MB_CREATE_GOODS_MOVEMENT.

This was mentioned in SAP Note-2603290 - M8147 during EWM / ECC posting with movement 241 - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA.

From S4 HANA 2025 FPS01, the delivery communication structure KOMDLGN is extended with the asset fields ANLN1 (main asset number) and ANLN2 (asset subnumber). These fields are populated from the goods movement data (MSEG) during delivery generation and persisted in the delivery item data (LIPS). During post goods issue, the same fields are mapped back from the delivery to the goods movement interface to ensure that the asset information is available for final posting.

LIPS table is extended to Store Asset Information.

A new delivery DOG3 is mapped to post asset posting against delivery.

Even in Synchronous Movement ERP2EWM class /SCWM/CL_EWM_GOODSMVT_SYNC & BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE, MIGO is enriched to pass asset information to EWM and conduct direct 241 Movement with material document.

Specify the handling unit number during synchronous production and process order consumption: 

Scope
• Enable for Handling Unit managed Production Staging Area (PSA) to define an exact Handling Unit (e.g. a sub-Handling Unit) for production consumption.
• Supported for synchronous goods movement postings in Production Order Confirmation API.
• Extension of existing BAPIs with Handling Unit parameter:
• BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR –Process Order
• BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_HDR – Production Order
• API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SR

The details and specifics of HU based consumption based posting is mentioned in SAP note-3702263 - Manufacturing Component Consumption from HU with synchronous goods movements using BAPIS and ODATA APIs

Below test case is replicated for Process Order Synchronous HU based Consumption. BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR is modified to pass HU information from S4 against process order/PMR.

The process order is consumed with HU- 803951 passed in BAPI-BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR

However, the Material document in S4 is not updated with HU.

As per SAP NOTE-3672337 - Enhance the Inventory Management objects with EWM Handling Unit information, only BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE & below Idocs are enhanced to update HU information in S4 material document.

EWM_HUIDENT, Type: /SCWM/DE_HUIDENT BAPI function modules that have been enhanced with warehouse handling unit information:

  • MAP2I_B2017_GM_ITEM_TO_IMSEG( BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE)
  • MP2E_IMSEG_TO_2017_ITEM_CREATE( BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE)
  • IDoc Segment that has been enhanced: E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE1

Lets Try HU based consumption in process order using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE

Bapi is executed and HU based posting is stored in Material document as well

Hence BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE can only update HU specific Posting in S4 MATDOC if customer intends to have this value stored. However, BAPI BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR can still be used if customer expectation is just to specific HU in Mnaufacturing order and update Confirmation parameters( PCNF, CNF). However, HU information will not be stored in MSEG table PP Confirmations Bapis.

Improved RF Receiving of Mixed HUs with Serial Numbers

Scope
• New button to maintain Serial Numbers for additional products in RF Receiving into a mixed HU that already contains Serial Numbers-managed products.
• Support serial number requirement on bin stock “C Serial Num. Req. in IM”
• Update of Inbound Delivery document with newly packed products/SNs.

Key Benefits
• Enhance the flow of inbound processing for mixed handling units
• Efficiently pack multiple products with serial number requirement into a Handling Unit (HU).

The other 2 below topics would be covered in next part of blog.

  • Extend the stock list during warehouse task creation
  • Support alternative units of measure for the warehouse stock migration object in SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit 

All tests are performed in S4 HANA 2025 FPS01 Sandbox system.

entry.title: SFTP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I understand in SAP Datasphere (SAP BDC) Trail version we can not create SFTP connection. How ever I tried to prepare steps in sequence as prerequisites to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere . Hope this will be helpful for understanding purpose.

I request community members, to let me know if I have any mistakes in below steps and if there is any way to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere in trail version.

From your Laptop Start Menu à Operational features

 

 

Check OpenSSH Client might already available

 

To install SSH Server click on Add a features

In search bar type Open --> OpenSSH Server --> Install

 

 

Goto Control Panel --> Click on Windows Defender Firewall --> Advanced Settings --> Inbound rules --> New Role

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From search bar search for Services

Try to find OpenSSH Server --> Right Click Properties

General Tab --> Startup Type = Automatic and click on Start        

Apply , OK and close window

 

 

 

Install WinSCP

From Google download WinSCP for windows --> go for official website

Run the .exe file for all users

Go with Default installation (Typical installation, Comander , install)

Select only check box Launch WinSCP --> Finish

As it will launch WinSCP

File Protocol = SFTP

 

SAP Cloud Connector

Log in SAP Cloud Connector: https://localhost:8443/

Add Subaccount

Goto Cloud and you can leave Host, Port, User and Password as blank

Click on Next

 

Configure Manually

 

 

 

 

In trail Version you can select Region as US East (VA) – AWS only. Other wise you can select any region. I copied Region and Subaccount from SAP BTP Cockpit as shown below screen shot. Normally in paid version you can select as per license

Login E-Mail/ID – This is of SAP BTP and Password also BTP.

 

SAP BTP Cockpit

 

 

Log on to SAP Cloud Connector à Select Subaccount crated in SAP Cloud Connector

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud Connector à SFTP

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How to get Host Key

Before Going to SAP Datasphere,

Goto Windows Power Shell and type below syntax

ssh-keyscan -p 22 sftp.XXX.XXX.X.X. hostkey.txt (in place of XXX.XXX.X.X – give your IP address)

Then it will open a notepad with Key.

But you need to take the last line in genearted Host Key from the note pad as like below

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

SAP Datasphere à Connections

 

 

 

 

 

Enter Host Key and give WinSCP Host details (SFTP)

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

 

 

We get below error in Free Trail Version

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Missing Steps

In SAP Datasphere (BDC) Trail version we don’t have access to System à Administration

 

 

 

If it gets success

Datasphere → BTP → Cloud Connector → SFTP is working.

entry.title: Re: Delay items part of curricula to become available to...
entry.content:
Hi @Gi,

My question is what are you trying to achieve with your training & the reporting on that training? A very good LMS Systems architect once said to me "You always start from where you want to finish, and work backwards from there".

If the five Items are individual parts of an overall Competency to show that the learner is competent to "maintain and calibrate X medical device".  Then "No", I would build each on top of each other (e.g. add each Item to the next Curricula, A, then AB, then ABC etc) as you/they/their managers/auditors need to know that they were competent to complete the task assigned of setting up and maintaining the "X" medical machine then a single Curricula is the way to go, but if they are standalone learning items that make up a part of an "onboarding Learning Path" say of:

  1. Welcome to the Company: Getting Started Essentials
  2. Your Role & Responsibilities: Understanding What You Do
  3. Company Policies, Culture & Code of Conduct
  4. Tools, Systems & Processes You Need to Know
  5. Health, Safety & Compliance Fundamentals

Then these can be in their own Curricula.  But again, in saying that I would probably recommend that they all be added to a Program where you force the order of completion (and also you can add some great additional content with Videos, HTML Cover Pages, Steps for soft skills steps e.g. "Have a conversation with your manager to set out your focus/KPI's for your first 3mths") and then have an assignment/s assigning the required curricula/s on their completion?  As this would be much simpler approach. 

It all depends on the outcome that you are after.  Different outcome options suit different methods of training delivery.

An example of where the outcome required, shapes how you set the system up comes to mind where we had internal trainers accredited to deliver Nationally recognised High Risk Training certification, but in our reporting we needed to know if a Learner achieved their Item Completion/certification via:

  1. Renewal of or Employed with Existing Certification - $0 training cost
  2. Completion of Training conducted by Internal Trainers - ~$500 training cost
  3. Completion of Training conducted by External Trainers - ~$1,500 training cost   

So, we created three different Completion Statuses for Credit for the Item Type CERT that allowed us to generate learning history reports for Periods of time (e.g. 6mths or Yearly) giving a rough breakdown of the High Risk Training costs to the business showing the benefit of the upfront costs of getting our trainers accredited to deliver the external training. 

Let me know your thoughts on the above.

Regards,

Nathan H.

entry.title: Re: Direct GR into Special Stock O for Different Vendor
entry.content: Direct GR from Vendor A into subcontracting stock O for Vendor B is not supported. The correct process is: GR into your plant → transfer posting 541 → subcontracting consumption. This ensures compliance and proper integration with procurement and inventory management.

entry.title: Re: Movement type 305 to consider post to inspection stoc...
entry.content: Since you mentioned you are on EHP7 for ERP 6.0 and not using QM, the system has no standard way to put the receiving plant’s stock into inspection automatically. If you truly need Q stock without QM, the safest approach is a custom Z‑movement type or a post‑processing transfer posting. Be cautious with enhancements as changing stock type logic can affect valuation and reporting. If your business later decides to activate QM, the standard “post to inspection” flag will work automatically, so consider whether enabling QM (even minimally) might be cleaner than custom coding.

entry.title: Re: Reversing a Completed Outbound Delivery in EWM: How t...
entry.content:
To your question "Why the stock stays in the GI‑AREA", when you reverse PGI, the system moves the stock back from “issued” into the GI‑AREA (the staging area for outbound deliveries). Cancelling picking (or “release stock”) does not physically move the stock back to its original source bin. It simply removes the reservation/assignment so the stock is no longer tied to the delivery. This is because, the warehouse task that picked the stock has already been confirmed, so the system cannot “un‑confirm” it automatically. That is why the reversal only releases the stock in the GI‑AREA.

Having said that, the recommended approach is reverse PGI → stock goes back to GI‑AREA. Cancel picking (release stock) → stock is free but still in GI‑AREA. Execute a warehouse task to move it back to the original bin. If this happens often, you can design a small automation (e.g., via EWM process control or custom logic) to trigger a “return to bin” task after cancellation.

entry.title: Re: is there an alternative to SAP Note 448601 ?
entry.content:
The requirement you have described relates to SAP Note 448601, but since you have found it incompatible with your current system landscape, the best course of action is to raise this directly with SAP Support. Notes of this type are often version‑specific, and SAP can advise whether there is a successor note, a correction instruction, or an alternative approach applicable to your exact release and installed components.
Given that this involves system compatibility and potentially corrective measures, I would recommend opening a customer incident in the SAP ONE Support Launchpad rather than relying on community input. That way, you will receive guidance tailored to your environment and confirmation of the correct note or workaround.

This way you acknowledge the issue, explain why the forum may not be the right place for a definitive answer, and point them toward SAP directly for resolution.

entry.title: Re: PO accruals and Budget consumption
entry.content:
Why it happens is because, Availability Control (AVAC) checks both commitments (POs) and actuals (FI/CO postings). When you post an accrual against the PO, the system interprets it as an actual expense on the WBS element. That is why you see “double consumption”: once for the PO commitment, and again for the accrual posting.

This is standard system behavior. Accruals are actuals, so they reduce budget. SAP does not provide a universal “fix note” to suppress accruals from AVAC. Instead, the recommended approach is configuration or process adjustment. What you can consider is to exclude the cost elements used for accrual postings from AVAC in the Availability Control Ledger. This way, accruals won’t consume budget, but commitments and true actuals will. In transaction OKOB / OKO6 (depending on release), adjust the ledger settings. Exclude the accrual cost elements from budget checks. This prevents accrual postings from reducing WBS budget.

entry.title: Re: SLED planning PPDS-Order conversion
entry.content:
When you convert a planned order to a production order in RRP3, the order is technically created in ECC/S4, but the batch assignment is not automatically transferred. Standard integration only passes quantities, dates, and pegging relationships,  not the detailed batch proposal. Batch determination in execution is designed to run again in S/4HANA PP at order release, which is why you see the system ignoring the PP/DS batch suggestion.

Activate CIF settings for batch transfer and implement a BAdI during order conversion to ensure pegged batch numbers are copied into the production order. This way, you won’t need to re-run batch determination in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Employee History Rule – Custom MDF Objec...
entry.content:
Hi @ln000 

Yes, there are known issues about using MDF in employee history.

I would need to test a bit more based on recent enhancements but in a nutshell and as far as I know from my latest testing: 

- there needs to be a record in your mdf for each employee as of the start of your varpay program start date for things to work. 

- If you map a varpayemphist to an MDF field you will have issues in the records retrieved when you run an "import employee history from EC" job. For example if an employee terminates it will not pick up the exact records you want.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: How do l implement a user exit or badi to retrigger r...
entry.content: The challenge is that the standard release strategy logic only retriggers on increases (net price, net value, quantity). If you want it to retrigger on decreases as well, you need to extend the release strategy determination logic with a custom enhancement. Implement logic in BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST or workflow-related BAdIs via the Custom Logic app in Public Cloud. In your code, explicitly check for both increases and decreases in net price, net value, or quantity, and reset the release strategy accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Selection production version during the order creatio...
entry.content:
Why Simi-001 picks L002 but Simi-002 defaults to L001 is because, for Simi-001, both validity and lot size conditions allow L002, so the system matches the parent’s version. For Simi-002, either the validity period or lot size of L002 does not match, or the customizing sequence prioritizes L001. That’s why the system consistently falls back to L001.

Check the following:

  • Validity dates of Simi-002’s L002 version — is it active on the order date?
  • Lot size ranges — does L002 cover the order quantity?
  • Selection strategy (OPPQ) — is L002 prioritized for BOM usage 1?
  • Consistency of production version assignment — ensure both phantoms have versions defined correctly in C223/C223N.
